title: Niphobolus nummulariifolius
intro: Niphobolus nummulariifolius, also known as Saptaparna or the coin-leafed Polypodium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, skin diseases, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Niphobolus nummulariifolius include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, Niphobolus nummulariifolius is commonly used to prepare herbal formulations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Niphobolus nummulariifolius is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Niphobolus is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical regions. Niphobolus nummulariifolius is native to tropical and subtropical parts of Asia and Africa. The exact classification of this species is still subject to ongoing research and updates in the field of botany.

properties: Niphobolus nummulariifolius is a plant with various therapeutic properties. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antipyretic properties.
